title: Example Site for Core 30
content_md:
# Example Site for Core 30

- Code: e4ca2c32
- Source: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=e4ca2c32591f44e8b2c2361c09b12e9b
- Video: https://www.loom.com/share/47352499858448418d2eee5ee3a431f5?hide_owner=true&hide_share=true&hide_title=true&hideEmbedTopBar=true
- Duration: 6m 22s

## Description

_(no description)_

transcript.text: is the example site. This example site is for a plumber in Gary, Indiana. Based on the web address,
you can probably tell that we built this example site with lovable. So a few things I want to point
out. We have obviously the title tag, which has the city name and the primary category. The h1 tag
also has the city name and primary category. But then we also start focusing on goal completion
since every user will see the h1. Beyond that, the immediate content that we have here above the
fold is all focused on goal completion. Why is someone in Gary, Indiana looking for a plumber
and what can we tell them about this plumbing business to convince them to make the call?
Notice that we don't talk about how many years we've been in business. We don't talk about anything
about how we're licensed and insured. We don't talk about anything like that. That's very obvious.
It's all about the user, what they need, why they're looking for this, and how we can help them
with that need. So scrolling just beyond that, well, actually, before we go down, obviously,
we have a click to call here. This is also a click to call. Clearly, you'd replace this with a company
name and your logo. The nav bar, very, very simple home services about us contact. That's all we have.
Schedule service for here, it goes to contact. If you have a different form or something like that,
obviously, you can send them there instead. Yet another click to call button. Yet another
click to call. We want to make it super clear how they can click to call. Okay, then right below
this, we have why Gary homeowners call us first. Again, remember, we're focusing on goal
completion, what we can do for them. Then immediately below that, what Gary customers say. So here's
where we embed the GBP reviews. Social proof is massively, massively important for this.
If we had awards, if we had mentions, if we had sponsored the local softball team,
we would put those logos somewhere in here to show we're an upstanding member of the community.
We care about the community, things like that. Okay, then right after this, we're going straight
into H2s for the primary H2s for the secondary categories. So you have drainage service,
link to more information about drainage service, bathroom remodeler, link to more information
about bathroom modelers. Those are the two secondary categories here. Then we have the
core services we care a lot about, water heat replacement, link to a page about that,
main drain line replacement, link to a page about that. Then we have the link to all services.
This is the same URL that the navbar links to. I'll show you that in a second.
Then we have a link to all the locations pages. This is where we would go to build
geographical relevance. Below that, we have an embed for the Google business profile.
A last final call to action. And then we have the footer. The footer includes, again, logo,
company name, phone number, address, email address, social profiles, repeats these links,
privacy policy terms of service. Super simple. Super simple. Okay. So let me show you for,
so this is a service, obviously, not a secondary category, but it's a service we care a lot about.
So I click on that link and I get brought to this sort of hub page about water heat replacement.
And you can see I have a bunch of sub services under the water heat replacement service.
That's because these are building up topical relevance for water heat replacement.
Okay. So if I come back under main drain line replacement, you can see the same thing.
These are services that we really, really care about. So we're going to build sub services
underneath them. We could also build a FAQ section down here if we wanted to. That would be fine
also would have no issues if you wanted an FAQ to get those service pages in.
So bathroom remodeler. This is our secondary category. And then here you can see we have
links to all of our services that live under that and drainage service again.
Here we have links to all of the drainage service underneath that we have them here too.
Now, if we go to the actual services page, which is linked to here, here and on the footer here,
I'll go to the same place. What we see is secondary category, all the services under
that secondary category, secondary category, all the services under that secondary category,
core service, the sub services under the core service, another core service, sub services
there. And now we have the primary category with all the services that we didn't want to link to
from the home page. So these are all of the services underneath the primary category.
So this website, it looks pretty small. It looks pretty simple. It's actually close to 60 pages
in 60 total pages. Oh, and let me show you the locations page. So if I hit view all locations,
Northwest Indiana, fine, whatever. So this page isn't designed to rank. All it's designed to do is
provide a hub for all of the geographic location pages to sit. So if we wanted to rank Plummer
Market Park, and if you go to the how to build geographical relevance section, you'll see
how we decide which one of these we do. It's going to be based on which locations on the local rank
map are four, five and six and have reasonable demographics. Then we're going to zoom into the
Google Maps and find a geographical landmark near that four, five or six and write content about
it. This content doesn't exist. It's all just call for service. But you know, we would build this
content out if we needed the geographical relevance. So the articles, so if we had one from Arquette
Park, this article would link to the primary category or core service page we're trying to
support. So if we're trying to rank plumbing, we would link back to the homepage from this page.
If we're trying to rank water heat replacement, we would link to the water heat replacement page
from this page. But we can't link out from the GBP landing page to all of these because there'd
be way too many. So anyway, hopefully that makes sense. Any questions, any issues, post it in the
group and we'll get them answered. This page also has schema. And yeah, so let me know any issues
you find with this. We can adjust it, make any corrections. These are all obviously AI generated
images. The content on the GBP landing page was written with our process for copywriters. The
rest of the content was just written by lovable, which is not great. So that not what I would
recommend doing. But that's what we did for this example site. So enjoy.

summary: The lesson explains how to create a simple yet effective website for a plumber in Gary, Indiana. The site focuses on user needs and goal completion by prominently featuring call-to-action buttons and relevant services.
key_points: Focus on user needs and goal completion
Keep navigation simple with essential links only
Build topical relevance through sub-service pages
